questions.scenario: Terraform already created two Linux VMs. The next pull request must install nginx, write `/etc/nginx/nginx.conf`, and keep a systemd service running inside those VMs. Which tool owns this change best?

options.text: Ansible, because the change configures packages, files, and services inside existing machines.
Terraform, because every line inside a Linux VM should be managed as a cloud resource.
Manual SSH, because service configuration is too small to review as code.
DNS, because nginx receives web traffic.

questions.explanation: Terraform is a good fit for infrastructure objects. Ansible is the better fit for repeatable operating system configuration inside existing machines.
